text.p: The State Emergency Service advises that people should:
* Don't drive, ride or walk through flood water.
* Keep clear of creeks and storm drains.
* Stay indoors, away from windows, while storms are nearby.

warning-info.text: The next warning is due to be issued by 7:05 pm.

text.p: Check https://www.bom.gov.au/weather-and-climate/warnings-and-alerts. Warnings are also available through TV and Radio broadcasts or call 1300 659 210. The Bureau and State Emergency Service would appreciate warnings being broadcast regularly.

forecast-period.text: Severe thunderstorm approaching the Yorke Peninsula

text.p: Weather Situation: A trough moving east combines with an unstable airmass to trigger severe thunderstorms in Gulf St Vincent. These are expected to continue throughout the afternoon as they approach the Yorke Peninsula.

Severe thunderstorms are likely to produce heavy rainfall that may lead to flash flooding in the warning area over the next several hours. Locations which may be affected include Maitland, Kadina and Ardrossan.

Severe thunderstorms are likely to produce heavy rainfall that may lead to flash flooding in the warning area over the next several hours. Locations which may be affected include Maitland, Kadina and Ardrossan.
